page: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage server optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, featuring 48 virtual CPUs (24 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 3.1 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is its massive local storage capacity of 335,520 GB delivered via HDD, paired with a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including large block read and write operations, consistently register in the average tier. Database operations and Redis SET throughput also perform at average levels, alongside LLM inference speeds for small and medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3en.12xlarge offers high resource density for bulk storage workloads, leveraging HDD technology to deliver massive capacity without the premium cost of solid-state drives. Given its hardware profile, this server is highly suited for storage-intensive workloads, large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, and data-dense database operations that require high network throughput and substantial local capacity rather than peak single-core CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage server built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 48 vCPUs (24 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores) running at 3.1 GHz and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It is equipped with a massive 335,520 GB of local HDD storage and a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the average tier, while single-core performance represents a tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server provides a cost-effective solution for high-capacity storage needs by utilizing HDDs instead of solid-state media. It is best suited for data-intensive workloads, distributed file systems, and large-scale data warehousing where massive local capacity and high network throughput are required.

tagline: A dense storage server featuring massive local HDD capacity and high network bandwidth for data-intensive and distributed file system workloads.

Massive local storage capacity of 335,520 GB utilizing HDD technology
High baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps optimized for data transfer
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Tradeoff of weaker single-core performance in exchange for dense storage capacity
Best suited for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and bulk storage
